metadata.title: Japan eCTD Submission Requirements: Implementation Guide v1.6.0 and Submission Formats Specification v1.3.1

source_files.note_on_filename: FILE MISLABELED — actual content is 'ICH Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) v4.0 Implementation Guide in Japan v1.6.0' (Appendix 1), Provisional Translation as of March 2025
FILE MISLABELED — actual content is 'Specification for Submission Formats for eCTD in Japan v1.3.1' (Appendix 2), Provisional Translation as of March 2025

metadata.deduplication_note: No prior coverage of Japan eCTD v4.0 implementation rules found in output-v2/, output-v3/, or output-v4/. Extracted fresh.
metadata.precedence_note: Both documents take precedence over their respective ICH parent documents (ICH IG and ICH SSF) where descriptions differ. The Japan-specific rules in this extraction supplement ICH M8 eCTD requirements.

rules.rule_text: An eCTD application submitted to PMDA must consist of three components only: (1) a Folder structure per section 5 of the Implementation Guide; (2) the eCTD v4.0 XML message (submissionunit.xml); and (3) Files. Folders or files not described in the Implementation Guide, and XML elements or attributes not described in the Implementation Guide, must not be included in the eCTD submitted to the Regulatory Authority.
rules.source_section: Section 3.1 — Components that should be included for eCTD application; Section 3.2 — Components not to be included
rules.verbatim_quote: Components that should be included for eCTD application are shown below. Folder / eCTD v4.0 XML message / File... The following component shall not be included in the eCTD to be submitted to the regulatory authority. 1) Folders or files not described in this document 2) XML elements and attributes not described in this document
rules.exceptions: None stated. All four file types within the 'Files' component are permitted: ICH CTD document files, Japan-specific document files, electronic study data, and non-referenced files (e.g., sha256.txt checksum file).

rules.rule_text: There are two permitted submission methods for eCTD in Japan: Method 1 (submission via the electronic study data system/gateway) and Method 2 (physical delivery to the PMDA reception counter, including by mail). For the first version of a new application, the applicant may use either Method 1 or Method 2. For all subsequent submissions (revisions) after the first version, only Method 1 is permitted.
rules.source_section: Section 3.3 — Matters related to submission; Section 3.3.1 — Submission method via the electronic study data system
rules.verbatim_quote: When submitting the first version of the data attached to the marketing application, submit it through either Method 1 or Method 2. In any other situation than the first version submission, it is only allowed through submission by Method 1.
rules.exceptions: Method 2 (physical delivery) is permitted for first-version submission only.

rules.rule_text: Japan's eCTD v4.0 defines three eCTD types for first-version submission using Method 1: Type a) — the main eCTD containing CTD document data and electronic study data from one eCTD v4.0 XML message; Type b) — the eCTD submitted by referring only to the electronic study data from 1 eCTD v4.0 XML message; Type c) — the eCTD submitted by referring only to CTD notification data from 1 eCTD v4.0 XML message. For Method 2 (first version), submit Type b) prior to Type c).
rules.source_section: Section 3.3.1 — Three eCTD types
rules.verbatim_quote: Of the following 3 eCTD types, either submit a) or submit b) and c). a) eCTD to be submitted by referring to the material specified by 'About guidelines for creating the data that should be attached for new drug marketing approval application'... b) eCTD to be submitted by referring to only the electronic study data from 1 eCTD v4.0 XML message among a) above. c) eCTD to be submitted by referring to the data specified by the CTD notification from 1 eCTD v4.0 XML message among a) above.
rules.exceptions: Method 1: submit type a), OR types b) and c). Method 2: type b) must be submitted before type c). All revisions must use Type a) only.

rules.rule_text: When delivering eCTD physically to the PMDA reception (Method 2), the applicant must: (1) create a cover letter for eCTD using Format 1 and submit it in writing; (2) include the electronic file of the cover letter (cover.pdf) in the m1/jp folder of the eCTD; (3) use DVD-R/RW or BD-R/RE (including multi-layer disks) as the submission medium in principle; (4) attach a label to the electronic medium containing at minimum: eCTD reception number, application category, application date, data submission date, brand name, applicant name, media sequence number/total count, submission sequence number, submission type of eCTD application, and remarks.
rules.source_section: Section 3.3.2 — Method to deliver to the reception of PMDA
rules.verbatim_quote: To deliver the eCTD to the reception of PMDA, create a cover letter for eCTD (format 1) to submit it in writing, and include it in eCTD, too. In this case, an electronic file of the cover letter (cover.pdf) shall be included in 'm1/jp'... In principle, the medium to be submitted shall be DVD-R/RW or BD-R/RE (each of which includes a multi-layer disk). If you wish to submit it in any other medium, consult PMDA in advance.
rules.exceptions: Alternative medium (other than DVD/BD) may be used if consulted with PMDA in advance.

rules.rule_text: The eCTD application must operate correctly in the standard review environment announced by the Regulatory Authority, as described in Appendix 2 (JP SSF). Applicants must notify the Regulatory Authority of the environment in which they confirmed operation. Plug-in software for perusal of the eCTD is not permitted in principle; this does not apply to plug-in software used for document creation.
rules.source_section: Section 3.4 — Operation requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: When making an eCTD application, you must guarantee that the functions (display, link, etc.) required by the Appendix 2 will work considering the standard environment for review to be announced by the Regulatory Authority. Applicants should notify the Regulatory Authority of the environment in which they have confirmed the operation... Also, the use of any plug-in software required for perusal is not permitted in principle. However, this does not apply to plug-in software used for creation.
rules.exceptions: If the RA cannot confirm correct operation, the applicant may be requested to replace the eCTD. Plug-in restriction applies only to perusal, not creation software.

rules.rule_text: All documents to be submitted must be included in eCTD as electronic files. Paper documents (signed/sealed pages) must be scanned and saved as electronic files in the eCTD. A statement confirming correct scanning must be prepared and included in Module 1 Section 3, to be submitted upon request from the Regulatory Authority. It is not permitted to submit an eCTD intended for bidirectional communication (as described in ICH IG), nor an eCTD that is associated with more than one submission or provides forward compatibility from v3.2.2 to v4.0.
rules.source_section: Section 3.5 — Requirements for using eCTD
rules.verbatim_quote: When making an eCTD application, you must submit all the documents to be submitted, which should be included in eCTD, in electronic files. Scan the relevant page signed or signed and sealed, etc. on a paper medium, replace it with the corresponding page on the electronic medium and save it and include it in eCTD... Note that you must not submit an eCTD intended for bidirectional communication described in ICH IG, and an application associated with more than one submission and forward compatibility from v3.2.2 to v4.0 to the regulatory authority.
rules.exceptions: Deviation from the document handling requirements is permitted only if confirmed with the RA in advance, with a deviation document included in Module 13 of Module 1.

rules.rule_text: Electronic signatures may be included in eCTD only when the target of indication of the signatory's intention is not the Regulatory Authority (e.g., for internal document approval, outsourcing contracts). Any electronic signature for which signer verification is required by the Regulatory Authority upon perusing the relevant data is not permitted; if such a signature is detected, the applicant may be requested to remove it.
rules.source_section: Section 3.6 — Handling of electronic signatures
rules.verbatim_quote: When electronically signing the data to be submitted to the regulatory authority, if the target of indication of your intention by the relevant act of the signature is not the regulatory authority (for example, internal document approval, a contract with an outsourcing company, etc.), then you may submit eCTD containing the electronically signed file. However, any electronic signature, for which signer verification is required by the regulatory authority upon perusing the relevant data, is not permitted.
rules.exceptions: Electronic signatures for internal purposes (not requiring RA signer verification) are permitted.

rules.rule_text: In addition to the ICH Controlled Vocabulary (ICH CV), the Japan-specific controlled vocabulary (JP CV) must be used in eCTD v4.0 submissions. The JP CV is provided within the eCTD v4.0 domestic implementation package. Each code list is version-controlled through the JP OID Listing; applicants must use the version that meets the 'Available Application' conditions in the JP OID Listing. Codes with a 'Retired' status in a code list version must not be used.
rules.source_section: Section 3.7 — Controlled vocabulary; Section 3.7.1 — Code List Versioning; Section 3.7.2 — Code Value Status
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to the controlled vocabulary specified by ICH (hereinafter referred to as 'ICH CV'), the controlled vocabulary specific to Japan (hereinafter referred to as 'JP CV') specified separately shall be used. JP CV is provided by the eCTD v4.0 domestic implementation package... However, in the case of a code list that does not have a 'Status' column, the status of all codes listed is considered to be 'Active.'
rules.exceptions: Code lists without a Status column are treated as having all Active codes.

rules.rule_text: For each eCTD application, a checksum file (sha256.txt) containing the SHA-256 checksum for the eCTD v4.0 XML message (submissionunit.xml) must be submitted. Additionally, the SHA-256 checksums of all individual submitted files (excluding the checksum file itself) must be included in the eCTD v4.0 XML message. Within the XML, the integrityCheckAlgorithm attribute value must be 'sha256' and the integrityCheck value must be in SHA-256 format.
rules.source_section: Section 4 — Security; Section 4.1 — Checksums
rules.verbatim_quote: In eCTD application, submit a file containing the checksum for the eCTD v4.0 XML message (sha256.txt) (hereinafter referred to as the 'checksum file'), and include the checksums of the individual submitted files except for the checksum file in the eCTD v4.0 XML message... The value must be 'sha256'. [for integrityCheckAlgorithm]... The checksum of the file referred to by this Document. The value must be in the sha256 format.
rules.exceptions: None stated. SHA-256 is mandatory; no alternative checksum algorithms are permitted.

rules.rule_text: The eCTD folder structure for Japan must follow these rules: (1) the first-level folder name must be the eCTD reception number; (2) the second-level folder name must be the submission sequence number; (3) CTD module folders (m1, m2, m3, m4, m5) must be placed at the third level; (4) folders for each individual report must be created within Module 4 and Module 5; (5) empty folders (folders that have no files to store due to document/file reuse) must not be created; (6) electronic study data must be placed under the m5 folder per ICH IG.
rules.source_section: Section 5.1 — Basic folder structure; Section 5.2 — Naming Conventions
rules.verbatim_quote: The first level folder name shall be the eCTD reception number, and the second level folder name shall be the submission sequence number. The folder of each section of CTD ('m1', 'm2', 'm3', 'm4' and 'm5') shall be placed at the third level. However, do not create any lower folder or folder not having any file to be stored in the folder at the third level or lower.
rules.exceptions: If there is no file to be stored due to reuse of the document or file, do not create any folder for the corresponding CTD number.

rules.rule_text: The eCTD v4.0 XML message must use UTF-8 character encoding. The OID of the implementation guide specified in the receiver.device.id.item@root attribute must be a version meeting the 'Available Applications' conditions in the JP OID Listing. The receiver.device.id.item@identifierName attribute value must be 128 characters or less. Elements must not contain dummy values or empty values as attributes; elements other than integrityCheck must not have content other than child elements.
rules.source_section: Section 7.1 — Character encode; Section 7.2 — Message header; Section 7.3 — Handling of elements and attributes not having any value
rules.verbatim_quote: Use UTF-8... The receiver.device.id.item@identifierName attribute value shall be 128 characters or less... do not let any elements except for the integrityCheck element have any other contents than the child element. Also, do not submit any integrityCheck element having no contents or integrityCheck element having dummy value as the content. As for the attribute, do not submit any attribute having a dummy value or empty value.
rules.exceptions: The integrityCheck element may have content other than child elements (the checksum value). The Regulatory Authority does not use the receiver.device.id.item@identifierName attribute value.

rules.rule_text: A Submission Unit will be rejected by PMDA if any of the following conditions are present: (1) multiple submissionUnit elements are provided; (2) the submissionUnit element is not provided; (3) the submissionUnit.title@value attribute has 1001 characters or more; (4) when submitting the first version, the component element is not provided.
rules.source_section: Section 7.4.2.3 — submissionUnit XML elements and attributes; Business Rules - General
rules.verbatim_quote: The Submission Unit, which falls under any of the following, will be rejected. Multiple submissionUnit elements are provided. / The submissionUnit element is not provided. / The title@value attribute value has 1001 characters or more. / When submitting the first version, the component element is not provided.
rules.exceptions: The submissionUnit.statusCode element is not used to withdraw a Submission Unit; withdrawal requires consultation with the RA.

rules.rule_text: Documents submitted in a previous eCTD application may be reused in a new application if EITHER of these conditions is met: (1) both the Submission Unit defining the document to be reused and the Submission Unit containing the documentReference element belong to the same application; OR (2) all three of the following apply: the application defining the document to be reused has already been approved, both the source and referencing applications are submitted as original per eCTD v4.0 specifications, and the source application dossier is within the prescribed document retention period. File reuse (8.2) is permitted on the same conditions; additionally, the Submission Units must belong to the same application, and the first layer folder name must not be included in the reference@value attribute.
rules.source_section: Section 8.1 — Document Reuse; Section 8.2 — File Reuse
rules.verbatim_quote: 1) Both the Submission Unit definining the Document to be reused and the Submission Unit that contains the documentReference element that references the Document to be reused belong to the same application. 2) All of the following conditions shall be satisfied: The application defining the Document to be reused has already been approved. Both the application defining the Document to be reused and the application containing the documentReference element referencing the Document to be reused are submitted as original in accordance with the eCTD v4.0 specifications. The application dossier defining the Document to be reused is subject to document retention by the regulatory authority and is within the prescribed retention period.
rules.exceptions: File reuse (Section 8.2) is not permitted for electronic study data.

rules.rule_text: Life cycle management for eCTD v4.0 in Japan uses the differential submission method: after the first version, only new or changed information must be submitted in each revision. However, certain information must be resubmitted each time regardless of whether it changed, due to the nature of the information or standards specifications. The submission sequence number for each revision must equal the previous sequence number plus 1; and the submission UUID, eCTD reception number, and submission code must be identical to those stated in the first version.
rules.source_section: Section 10.1 — Overview; Section 10.3.3 — eCTD v4.0 XML message structure for revision
rules.verbatim_quote: In life cycle management of eCTD v4.0, the differential submission method is adopted for the eCTD v4.0 XML messages and files in principle. In an eCTD submission (package) after submitting the first version, the applicant shall submit the information on new submission or change to the existing information only. However, keep in mind that there is a case where unchanged information must also be submitted each time in terms of the nature of the information or the specification of the standards.
rules.exceptions: Some information categories must be resubmitted unconditionally even without changes.

rules.rule_text: The mandatory files for first-version eCTD submission are: (1) eCTD v4.0 XML message (submissionunit.xml); (2) checksum file (sha256.txt); and (3) the content files for the first version. For revision submissions, the mandatory files are: (1) eCTD v4.0 XML message (submissionunit.xml); (2) checksum file (sha256.txt); and (3) the files to be submitted for the relevant revision.
rules.source_section: Section 10.2.2 — File for first version submission; Section 10.3.2 — Files for revision
rules.verbatim_quote: The following files must submit: eCTD v4.0 XML Message (submissionunit.xml) / Checksum file (sha256.txt) / Files to be submitted with the first version... The following files must be submitted. eCTD v4.0 XML message (submissionunit.xml) / Checksum file (sha256.txt) / The files to be submitted for the relevant revision
rules.exceptions: For Method 2 first-version submission with eCTD type b), only electronic study data files are submitted; for type c), only CTD notification data files are submitted.

rules.rule_text: All revisions to an existing eCTD must use eCTD Type a) regardless of the submission package type. Context of Use (CoU) Keywords (CoU Keyword) for an already submitted Context of Use cannot be added, replaced, or deleted during revision. To change the context group association of a document at revision, the existing Context of Use must be deleted and a new Context of Use created, after which the document can be reused or re-submitted.
rules.source_section: Section 10.3 — Requirements for eCTD revision; Section 10.3.3 — eCTD v4.0 XML message structure for revision
rules.verbatim_quote: For eCTD for revision, use Type a) indicated in 3.3.1 of this document regardless of the submission package... You cannot add, replace or delete any COU Keyword for the already submitted Context of Use. As for an already submitted Document, if you want to associate it with a different context group, delete the existing Context of Use, which refers to the relevant Document, and provide a new Context of Use.
rules.exceptions: None stated. These constraints apply throughout the entire life cycle of the eCTD.

rules.rule_text: Once a Document has been submitted in an eCTD, it cannot be changed or deleted throughout the life cycle of the eCTD, except to correct a typo in the document title (corrected by changing the document.title@value attribute value). Updating a Document title applies to all Submission Units referring to the same document.id@root value. Two situations prohibit Document title updates: (1) updating the title of a document belonging to another application, and (2) where the title update changes the positioning of the document rather than correcting a typo. Similarly, once a Keyword Definition is submitted, it cannot be changed or deleted except to modify the notation of the displayed character string indicated by the displayName@value attribute.
rules.source_section: Section 10.3.3 — Document and Keyword Definition immutability; Section 10.3.4.2 and 10.3.4.3
rules.verbatim_quote: You cannot change or delete the once submitted Document throughout the life cycle of eCTD except to correct the typo in the title... You cannot change or delete the once submitted Keyword Definition throughout the life cycle of eCTD except to modify the notation of the displayed character string indicated by the displayName@value attribute.
rules.exceptions: Title typo correction and Keyword Definition displayName notation changes are permitted throughout the lifecycle.

rules.rule_text: Multiple lifecycle operations (new submission, replacement, removal, update) must not be performed simultaneously within the same Submission Unit for a single Context of Use (identified by UUID), Review/Document (identified by UUID), or Keyword Definition (identified by a combination of code and codeSystem attribute values). For example, for one Context of Use, both a contextOfUse element for removal (statusCode='suspended') and a contextOfUse element for updating the display order must not be provided in the same Submission Unit.
rules.source_section: Section 10.3.6 — Important notes for revision
rules.verbatim_quote: Multiple operations must not be performed simultaneously within the same Submission Unit for one Context of Use/Review/Document specified by UUID and one Keyword Definition specified by a combination of code and codeSystem attribute values. For example, for one Context of Use, the contextOfUse element for remove and the contextOfUse element for updating the display order must not be provided in the same Submission Unit at the same time.
rules.exceptions: None stated. If multiple operations on the same entity are needed, they must be split into separate Submission Units.

rules.rule_text: If eCTD v3.2.2 is used for the first-version submission, revisions during the review period must also use eCTD v3.2.2; it is not permitted to make a revision using eCTD v4.0 during the review time period if the first version was submitted in v3.2.2. The same version of eCTD must be used from submission of the first version to completion of the life cycle. Additionally, it is not permitted to inquire about the leaf ID (etc.) of a document submitted using v3.2.2 or earlier from an application using v4.0 or later.
rules.source_section: Section 10.3.5 — Migration from eCTD v3.2.2
rules.verbatim_quote: If eCTD v3.2.2 is used when submitting the first version, you cannot make a revision using eCTD v4.0 during the review time period. Use the same version of eCTD from submission of the first version to completion of the life cycle. You are not allowed to inquire the leaf ID, etc. of the document submitted using the version of v3.2.2 or earlier from an application using the version of v4.0 or later.
rules.exceptions: None stated. Version locking applies for the entire lifecycle of a given application.

rules.rule_text: For the Context of Use that refers to electronic study data files, the following mandatory CoU Keywords must be assigned: (1) 'JP Study Data Category' code (applies to all electronic study data); (2) 'JP Analysis Type' code (applies to all electronic study data); (3) 'JP Terminology (Tabulation)' code for files under the 'sdtm' and 'sdtm_j' folders (SDTM data sets); (4) 'JP Terminology (Analysis)' code for files under the 'adam' and 'adam_j' folders (ADaM data sets). The study_id part of the study id_study title Keyword must match the name of the study ID folder (case-insensitive).
rules.source_section: Section 11 — Important notes for referring to the electronic study data from the eCTD v4.0 XML message
rules.verbatim_quote: Assign the CoU Keyword to the Context of Use, which refers to the electronic study data, as follows. [All electronic study data:] JP Study Data Category — Appropriate code; JP Analysis Type — Appropriate code. [SDTM data set (Files under 'sdtm' and 'sdtm_j' folders):] JP Terminology (Tabulation) — Appropriate code. [ADaM data set (Files under 'adam' and 'adam_j' folders):] JP Terminology (Analysis) — Appropriate code. The study id part of the study id_study title Keyword assigned to the Context of Use that refers to the electronic study data shall be the same as the name of the study ID folder. However, it is not necessary to match uppercase and lowercase letters.
rules.exceptions: Case-insensitive matching for study ID folder names is acceptable.

rules.rule_text: The file path after 'm5/datasets' for each electronic study data file must be unique across the application to enable validation. When submitting new electronic study data, the file path must not already be in use by an existing active Context of Use in the same lifecycle position. When replacing previously submitted electronic study data, the file path under m5/datasets of both the file referenced before and after replacement must match.
rules.source_section: Section 11 — Electronic study data note 7
rules.verbatim_quote: It is necessary that the file path after 'm5/datasets' in each electronic study data must be unique because the file to be validated for the electronic study data is uniquely identified... When replacing previously submitted electronic study data: The file path under 'm5/datasets' of both the file referenced by Context of Use before replacement and the file referenced by Context of Use after replacement must match.
rules.exceptions: A newly submitting Context of Use can refer to the same file path as a deleted Context of Use.

rules.rule_text: The following conditions must be simultaneously met between the Context of Use referencing electronic study data and the Context of Use referencing the corresponding clinical study report: (1) the code@code and code@codeSystem values of the study id_study title Keyword must be the same; (2) the code@code and code@codeSystem values of the Context of Use itself must match; (3) the CoU Keyword values for 'ICH Study Group Order' and 'indication' must match between the two Contexts of Use. File reuse (Section 8.2) is not permitted for electronic study data.
rules.source_section: Section 11 — Electronic study data notes 5 and 8
rules.verbatim_quote: 5)-1 The code@code and code@codeSystem attribute values of the study id_study title Keyword shall be the same between them. 5)-2 The following information shall match between the Context of Use that references the electronic study data and at least one of the Context of Use that references the clinical study report corresponding to it... The code@code and code@codeSystem attribute values of Context of Use / The CoU Keyword code@code and code@codeSystem attribute values below: ICH Study Group Order / indication... 8) When reusing a Document that references electronic study data, all of the following conditions must be met in addition to 8.1 of this document. The file reuse shown in 8.2 of this document is not permitted in the electronic study data.
rules.exceptions: When submitting the first version using Method 2 (eCTD type b), the study report context of use is not included; conditions 5)-1 and 5)-2 need not be met at that time, but must be met when submitting eCTD type c).

rules.rule_text: For the Context of Use referring to clinical study reports in Module 5 of the CTD, the ICH CV 'ICH Document Type' keyword must be assigned as follows: when the clinical study report is submitted as a single file, assign 'ich_document_type_2'; when the clinical study report consists of multiple files, assign an appropriate code from the E3-Reference column corresponding to the item number of the clinical study report for each file's Context of Use.
rules.source_section: Section 12.1 — CoU Keyword to be assigned to the clinical study report
rules.verbatim_quote: When submitting the clinical study report in one file: Assign 'ich_document_type_2' to the Context of Use, which refers to the relevant file. When submitting the clinical study report consisting of multiple files: For the Context of Use that refers to each file, assign an appropriate one of the Codes with the item number of the clinical study report provided in the column of 'E3-Reference'.
rules.exceptions: The ICH Document Type Keyword does not need to be assigned to the Context of Use that refers to electronic study data.

rules.rule_text: In addition to ICH IG validation rules, Japan-specific business rules described in the Implementation Guide apply to eCTD validation in Japan. For detailed validation requirements, applicants must refer to the list of check items published on the PMDA website and the operation manual for the eCTD v4.0 verification tool. eCTD v4.0 operation inquiries must be submitted to PMDA via email (ectd@pmda.go.jp); general questions (not product-specific) must be submitted through an industry organization such as JPMA.
rules.source_section: Section 13 — Validation rules; Section 14 — Inquiry
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to the validation rules provided in ICH IG, the business rules, etc., described in this document apply to the validation rules for eCTD application in Japan. For more specific details of validation for eCTD applications in Japan, refer to the list of check items published on the PMDA website (https://www.pmda.go.jp/) and the operation manual, etc., for the eCTD v4.0 verification tool.
rules.exceptions: Product-specific questions may be submitted directly to PMDA without going through an industry organization.

rules.rule_text: Module 1 of the eCTD for Japan must use the folder structure m1/jp in principle. Applicants may create sub-folders under the jp folder as necessary, but must not change the hierarchy up to m1/jp. The cover letter for eCTD (cover.pdf) must be stored in m1/jp. The file naming conventions for Module 1 documents are specified by PMDA: for example, 1.1-1 Table of contents = m1-01-01.pdf; 1.2 Marketing application form copy = m1-02-XX.pdf; 1.12-1 Attached submission data list (PDF) = m1-12-01.pdf; 1.12-2 Attached submission data list (Excel) = m1-12-02.xlsx.
rules.source_section: Section 15 — Appendix 1: Module 1 creation guidelines; Section 15.2 — Folder structure; Section 15.3 — File naming
rules.verbatim_quote: The folder structure and folder naming for Module 1 shall be the following folder structure in principle. [m1 > jp] The applicant may create folders under the 'jp' folder as necessary. However, do not change the hierarchy up to 'm1/jp'.
rules.exceptions: The file names in Section 15.3 are reference conventions; applicants may provide any name. The 1.1-1 table of contents submission is optional for eCTD applications.

rules.rule_text: Documents related to already approved pharmaceutical products included in Module 1 of CTD must be submitted using one of three methods: (1) include the files in the m1/jp folder and refer to them via the Context of Use with appropriate CTD heading; (2) reuse the Document from the approved application's eCTD and associate with the CTD heading via Context of Use; or (3) include the eCTD reception number of the already approved application in the Application Reference (id@root). If method 3 is used, those omitted documents must not also be provided via method 1 or 2 (no duplication). The submission method cannot be changed mid-lifecycle.
rules.source_section: Section 15.4 — How to submit the data related to the already approved pharmaceutical products
rules.verbatim_quote: 1) Include the relevant files in the 'm1/jp' folder, get them referred to from the Document, and associate them with the corresponding CTD headings using the Context of Use. 2) Reuse the Document of the approved application to associate it with the corresponding CTD heading using the Context of Use. 3) Include the eCTD reception number of the relevant already approved application in the id@root attribute of Application Reference. If the method of 3) above is adopted, any document related to already approved pharmaceutical product that are omitted by method of 3) must not be provided by Module 1 of CTD in duplicate by method of 1) or 2).
rules.exceptions: Documents that cannot be omitted under the simplification rules must be submitted via method 1) or 2). You are not allowed to change the submission method in the middle of the life cycle in principle.

rules.rule_text: Japan's eCTD cover letter (Format 1) must be submitted when delivering eCTD physically to PMDA. The cover letter must include: eCTD reception number, submission sequence number, application category, application date, generic name, brand name, modules included (with indication of whether electronic study data are included in Module 5 and whether inquiry responses are included in Module 1), type of electronic medium, number of submitted media, size of each medium, virus check statement, checksum of submissionunit.xml, operation confirmation environment (OS and PDF version), contact information for both regulatory representative and technical representative, and remarks.
rules.source_section: Format 1 — Cover letter for eCTD
rules.verbatim_quote: Format 1 (Cover letter for eCTD): eCTD reception number / Submission sequence number / Application category / Application date / Name [Generic Name / Brand name] / Modules, etc. to be included in this submission (Check the corresponding module, etc.) / Type of the electronic medium / Number of submitted media / Size (for each submitted medium) / Statement regarding virus check / Checksum of submissionunit.xml / Operation confirmation environment [OS: / PDF Version:] / Contact / Remarks
rules.exceptions: Cover letter is required only for physical delivery (Method 2). Electronic submissions via Method 1 do not require a separate cover letter.

rules.rule_text: Japan-specific PDF requirements for eCTD documents include: (1) recommended Japanese fonts are Unicode-supported MS Gothic, MS Mincho, Medium Gothic, and Fine Mincho; when using other Japanese fonts, sub-set embedding must be used; (2) font size for Japanese documents is 10.5pts in principle (8pts or larger for diagrams); (3) hypertext links within the same PDF and between different PDFs must be properly set up; links from Module 2 to Module 3 through 5 must be set up as much as possible; (4) no file-level security settings or password protection may be applied to individual eCTD files, with the exception that other security settings (printing, document change restrictions) may be applied to references in Modules 3, 4, and 5 if the content remains accessible without a password.
rules.source_section: Specification for Submission Formats for eCTD in Japan v1.3.1 — Sections 4.1, 4.1.1, 4.1.2, 4.3, 4.4
rules.verbatim_quote: The recommended Japanese fonts shall be Unicode-supported MS Gothic, MS Mincho or Medium Gothic and Fine Mincho... The font size of the text, which is to be used for a Japanese document, shall be 10.5pts in principle. However, use a readable size of the font for a diagram, etc... A hypertext links within the same PDF file) or between different documents (different PDF files) shall be properly set up to make the review efficient. Set up a hypertext links from Module 2 to Module 3 through 5 as much as possible... The applicant must not apply any file-level security settings or password protection to individual files of eCTD in principle.
rules.exceptions: Security settings (printing/document change restrictions) are permitted for Modules 3, 4, and 5 reference files if content remains accessible without password entry. Annotated CRFs may be created as PDF with annotations.

rules.rule_text: Documents included in eCTD that were created as paper media before March 2006 may be included as scanned PDF if readable; for any data created after March 2006, a PDF file must be created from an electronic file (text PDF in principle). If any file format other than PDF or Microsoft Office format is required for data other than electronic study data, the Regulatory Authority must be consulted in advance. This does not apply to eCTD XML instance files (index.xml, jp-regional-index.xml, submissionunit.xml, etc.), checksum files (md5.txt, sha256.txt), DTD, Schema, and stylesheets.
rules.source_section: Specification for Submission Formats for eCTD in Japan v1.3.1 — Sections 3 and 4.5
rules.verbatim_quote: Of the data included in eCTD, if it is necessary to submit any other data than the electronic study data in any other file format than the PDF format or the Microsoft Office format, then consult the Regulatory Authority in advance... Of the reports, etc. to be attached to Module 3, Module 4 and Module 5, you may include the data already created as paper media before March 2006 in eCTD if readable even in PDF other than that of the specifications provided in this document (PDF created through scanning, etc.). Create a PDF file from an electronic file for any data created after that (text PDF) in principle.
rules.exceptions: XML instance files, checksum files, DTD, Schema, and stylesheets are exempt from the PDF/Office format requirement.

rules.rule_text: Two Japan-specific file types must be submitted with every eCTD application dossier: (1) the patient list (case list) must be submitted in PDF format; the Regulatory Authority may additionally request Excel format during review, in which case the content must be consistent with the PDF and no contradiction is permitted; (2) the attached submission data list must be submitted in both PDF and Microsoft Excel format, with the Excel file containing the following 9 mandatory data items in one row per submission data entry: attached submission data number, title, author, period to conduct study, place to conduct study, report type (domestic/overseas), journal carrying the articles, whether evaluation or reference data, and whether electronic study data are submitted.
rules.source_section: Specification for Submission Formats for eCTD in Japan v1.3.1 — Sections 5.1 and 5.2
rules.verbatim_quote: The file format for the case list shall be the PDF format, but if it is judged that it is necessary to carry out a review, we may request you to separately submit it in the Microsoft Excel format... A list of attached submission data shall be submitted in the PDF format and the Excel format. Upon creating a list in the Excel format, accommodate the following items in 1 line so that data can be sorted and extracted. Items for the attached submission data list: 1. Attached submission data number 2. Title 3. Author 4. Period to conduct study 5. Place to conduct study 6. Report type (domestic, overseas) 7. The magazine carrying the articles 8. Whether it is evaluation data or reference data 9. Whether or not electronic study data are submitted
rules.exceptions: Patient list Excel submission is only required when specifically requested by the RA during review.
